payload.summary: Reviewed three amendments against the actual code in src/adapters/fs.rs. The CorruptRecord file field amendment (dfea2d32) identifies a genuine inconsistency where a function name is used instead of a file path. The two plan_hash backfill amendments (ecebda75 and c54fc6aa) describe behavior that is intentional and well-documented in the code — auto-population is deliberately avoided to prevent a dispatch/snapshot race condition (new rows) and ambiguous legacy entry relabeling (existing rows). Forcing auto-population would introduce the exact defects the code is designed to prevent.

votes.rationale: Verified: line 2840 uses file: "unique_terminal_replay_match" (a function name) while every other CorruptRecord in fs.rs uses actual file paths like "milestones/{id}/task-runs.ndjson". All three callers have milestone_id available. This is a genuine diagnostic inconsistency that makes it harder for operators to locate corrupt files. Simple fix with clear benefit.

The non-population is intentional and documented with a detailed comment (lines 3041-3049) explaining the dispatch-v1/snapshot-v2 race: if persist_plan() advances the snapshot between bead dispatch and record_task_run_start, auto-populating from the snapshot would stamp the entry with the newer plan hash even though the bead was dispatched under the older plan. This would cause false 'conflicting plan_hash' errors at completion time. The snapshot hash IS captured in snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation for provenance, and safe_plan_hash_backfill() fills plan_hash later when provably safe (plan unchanged). The amendment's proposed fix would reintroduce the race condition the code was specifically designed to prevent.

The code at lines 2609-2620 explicitly documents this as a 'deliberate deviation from Issue 3 acceptance criteria' with sound reasoning: snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation being None is ambiguous between (a) entry created before any plan existed and (b) legacy entry from older code that didn't record the field. These cannot be distinguished, so backfilling would risk silently relabeling a legacy entry with a newer plan version. This is the correct conservative choice — preferring correctness over completeness. All NEW entries always set snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation via snapshot_plan_hash(), so they are eligible for safe backfill. Only genuinely ambiguous entries are skipped, and these will naturally age out.
